title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives · Runtime Lens: Fragment Contract
summary: One projectile damages the same rock twice, both loops delete the same sprite, fragments recursively collide during creation, ship damage repeats every frame, and feedback arrives after state removal. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fragment contract increment in ordered single-resolution collision response. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.
Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-fragment-contract — Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-response-damage-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-response-damage-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_collision_response_damage_2(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-fragment-contract-yy7qff
# Competency: asteroids-fragment-contract.
# Case 9683: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_fragment_contract_yy7qff(collisions: int = 3, resolved_once: int = 3, invulnerability_ms: int = 1200, duplicate_damage: int = 0, fragments_spawned: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_yy7qff = "yy7qff-ffq7yy-9683"
    assert evidence_variant_yy7qff
    evidence_axes_yy7qff = (
        "playeryy7qff",
        "seed9683",
        "inputffq7yy",
        "frameyy7qff9683",
        "state9683yy7qff",
        "playtestffq7yy9683",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_yy7qff)) == 6
    if collisions < 0 or resolved_once < 0:
        return False, collisions, "response-domain"
    if resolved_once != collisions:
        return False, resolved_once, "response-count"
    if invulnerability_ms < 0 or duplicate_damage > 0:
        return False, duplicate_damage, "damage-window"
    if fragments_spawned < collisions:
        return False, fragments_spawned, "fragment-policy"
    assert resolved_once == collisions
    return True, fragments_spawned, "single-response"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fragment Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3314 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fragment Contract model · Theory Fragment Contract.
steps.why: Retrieving Fragment Contract through the concrete ordered single-resolution collision response case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fragment Contract model · Theory Fragment Contract, keep Fragment Contract connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fragment Contract model · Theory Fragment Contract, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Fragment Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fragment Contract model · Theory Fragment Contract, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 3314 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fragment Contract model · Theory Fragment Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Fragments can safely inherit every parent field.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-fragment-contract-yy7qff(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_fragment_contract_yy7qff\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resolved_once != collisions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_damage > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fragments_spawned < collisions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resolved_once == collisions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
